taskDescription: Arrange the layers encountered when accessing the heart from the outside in (superficial to deep).
paragraphs: Fibrous pericardium — the tough outer bag of dense connective tissue that anchors the heart to the diaphragm and sternum.
Parietal layer of serous pericardium — lines the inner surface of the fibrous pericardium.
Pericardial cavity — the potential space containing 15-50 mL of serous fluid for lubrication.
Visceral layer of serous pericardium (epicardium) — covers the surface of the heart directly.
Myocardium — the thick muscular wall of the heart, supplied by coronary arteries.
Endocardium — the smooth inner lining of the heart chambers.
